Notice Or Motion And Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of Matters Admitted In Plaintiff'S Rquests For Admissions (Set One)
Off Calendar Apr-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ESEQUIEL REYES' Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of Matters Admitted In Plaintiff'S Rquests For Admissions (Set One).

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion is moot since responses were served after the filing of this motion. However, the responses were late and all objections have been waived. Defendants' responses contain a litany of objections. To retain those objections, Defendants need to file a motion under the various discovery statutes for relief from waiver. Defendants are directed to file any such motion by April 29, 2024. If such motion is filed and granted, the parties shall meet and confer on the responses before another motion is filed. If the motion is denied, Defendants must provide complete responses without objections. Sanctions of $500 awarded to Plaintiffs.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One)
Off Calendar Apr-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ESEQUIEL REYES' Motion To Compel Responses, Without Objections, To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One).

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Joshua Kohanoff, Esq., of Quill & Arrow, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.
Vanessa Dao, Esq., of CLARK HILL LLP, (619) 557-0404, for Defendant.

The matter is not argued/contested.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. The motion is moot since responses were served after the filing of this motion. However, the responses were late and all objections have been waived. Defendants' responses contain a litany of objections. To retain those objections, Defendants need to file a motion under the various discovery statutes for relief from waiver. Defendants are directed to file any such motion by April 29, 2024. If such motion is filed and granted, the parties shall meet and confer on the responses before another motion is filed. If the motion is denied, Defendants must provide complete responses without objections. Sanctions of $500 awarded to Plaintiffs. At the court's query, counsel for Defendant requests to extend the deadline to file the motion mentioned above to May-08-2024. No objection is presented to the preceding. Counsel's request is granted.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
